regressions.description: nonGoal violation: bober-security-auditor subagent granted unrestricted Bash tool (via resolveRoleTools('evaluator',...) whose set includes 'bash' = full sh -c execution). Contract nonGoals[3]: 'Do not give the auditor subagent write/edit/bash tools.'
regressions.evidence: agents/bober-security-auditor.md:4-9 lists Bash; security-auditor-agent.ts:62 resolveRoleTools('evaluator',...); tools/index.ts:67 evaluator=['bash','read_file','glob','grep']; handlers.ts:87-121 unrestricted sh -c

generatorFeedback.description: Contract self-contradiction resolved the wrong way: generatorNotes said 'mirror code-reviewer tool set' but nonGoals[3] explicitly forbids bash. The nonGoal (backed by the spec assumption 'auditor subagent runs with read-only tools' and the architecture's securityConsiderations) must win.
generatorFeedback.expected: Auditor gets only read_file/glob/grep — the exact shape of ROLE_TOOLS['researcher-phase2']/curator (src/orchestrator/tools/index.ts:62-64). Change security-auditor-agent.ts:62 to resolve a bash-less role tool set (reuse researcher-phase2/curator or add an explicit auditor role) and remove Bash from agents/bober-security-auditor.md frontmatter + any body text implying shell use. Add a test asserting the tools passed to runAgenticLoop contain no 'bash' ToolDef.

summary: 7/7 criteria pass, suite 3958 green, but FAIL on critical nonGoal violation: auditor wired with unrestricted bash tool. Fix: bash-less read-only tool set + frontmatter, test-proven.
